t_ch: 看来…一场恶战在所难免了。
t_en: It seems like... a fierce battle will be inevitable.

t_ch: 大家，不用担心！缇安保护大家！
t_en: Don't worry! Trianne will protect everyone!

t_ch: 呵…眼睛看不见了啊……
t_en: Ha... My eyes can no longer see...

t_ch: 无妨…只要把这副身体置入阵眼……
t_en: No matter... All I have to do is to place this body at the core of the array...

t_ch: 为了翁法罗斯…给我陪葬吧，泰坦。
t_en: For the sake of Amphoreus... Die with me, Titan.

t_ch: 你究竟是什么人…？
t_en: Who exactly are you...?

t_ch: 好强…{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}说的是真的……
t_en: So powerful... {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} was right...

t_ch: 她人呢？火种还没拼好吗！
t_en: Where is she? Is the Coreflame still not pieced together yet!?

t_ch: 以尔魂息…祭火吧。
t_en: Offer your souls... to the fire.

t_ch: 不好…！
t_en: Not good...!

t_ch: 化作…死灰！
t_en: Be reduced to... ashes!

t_ch: 偷袭不成，就难再次得手了…可惜。
t_en: Since the ambush failed, it'll be difficult to find another opportunity... What a pity.

t_ch: 那刻夏老师？不…是{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}？
t_en: Professor Anaxa? No... It's {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 你终于来了，想死你了！
t_en: You're finally here! I missed you so much!

t_ch: 难缠。人子们，切勿动摇，逐个击破便是。
t_en: How annoying. Children of humanity, don't waver now. Let's just destroy them one by one.

t_ch: 呵呵，真要应接不暇了——战斗竟比思考累人得多哪。
t_en: Heh, I'm about to be overwhelmed. Fighting is so much more tiring than thinking.

t_ch: 各位，麻烦互相看好背后！
t_en: Everyone, watch each other's back!

t_ch: 啊呀，人子们…哪面是背来着？
t_en: Ahh, children of humanity... Which side is the back?

t_ch: 老师…不，泰坦，别开玩笑了…！
t_en: Professor — no, Titan, stop joking!

t_ch: 不容易…吾要精疲力尽了。
t_en: That was tough... I'm truly exhausted.

t_ch: 等等，不对…大家小心！
t_en: Wait, no... Look out, everyone!

t_ch: 别担心，小敌。这种情况也不是第一次啦，很久以前……
t_en: Don't worry, De. This isn't the first time he's been like this. A long time ago—

t_ch: 我没有在担心他。
t_en: I'm not worrying about him.

t_ch: 喔…好吧。但你有点沉默。
t_en: Oh... Alright. You're a little quiet, though.

t_ch: …失礼了，我不该这么粗鲁。那个男人能挺过去，我心里清楚。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, that was rude of me. What I meant was, I know that man can pull through for sure.

t_ch: 没关系啦，直接一点，才像是你的风格。
t_en: Hehe, it's alright! Being straightforward suits you better.

t_ch: 小敌，*我们*想问的是…你在{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的试炼里看到了什么？
t_en: Actually, De, there's something we are curious about... What did you see in {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s trial?

t_ch: …令我恐惧之事。
t_en: ...Something that terrifies me.

t_ch: 讽刺么？悬锋人的字典里本不该有「害怕」二字。
t_en: Isn't that ironic? There shouldn't even be a word for "fear" in the Kremnoan language.

t_ch: 你觉得…自己有机会战胜它吗？令你害怕的那样东西？
t_en: Do you think... you stand a chance? Against that thing that terrifies you?

t_ch: 这也在你们的布局中么？
t_en: Is this also part of your grand scheme?

t_ch: 什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 阿格莱雅知道白厄可能会失败……
t_en: Aglaea knew that Phainon might fall...

t_ch: 让我成为他的递补，你们原本就是这么打算的。
t_en: ...And she expected me to take his place. That was the plan all along, wasn't it?

t_ch: …嗯。看来你全都明白呀。
t_en: ...Yes. Looks like you know everything.

t_ch: 我并无谴责之意。逐火之旅不是儿戏，这我早有觉悟。
t_en: I have no intention of condemning you for it. The Flame-Chase Journey is not a game, and I have long understood this.

t_ch: 「纷争」陨落，黑潮的威胁再度加剧…倘若{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}也受到影响，后果不堪设想。
t_en: With the downfall of Strife and the ever-growing threat that the black tide poses, if {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} are also impacted, the ramifications will be dire.

t_ch: 是啊。它们是唯二尚存理智，还愿意与人类共存的泰坦了。
t_en: Mm-hmm. They are the only two Titans who are still lucid and willing to coexist with humanity.

t_ch: 可惜，完成试炼的关键不在于我。若想战胜那道恐惧，我需要其他悬锋族人的帮助。
t_en: Regrettably, I do not hold the key to completing the trial. If I wish to conquer that fear, I need the assistance of my fellow Kremnoans.

t_ch: 给我些时间，让我和族人对话…不会花上太久。
t_en: Give me some time to speak with my people... It won't take long, I promise.

t_ch: 噢，人多力量大，对吧？不过半神的试炼终究要落到个人身上……
t_en: Ah, I see — there is strength in numbers. But you must know that the trial to become a demigod is ultimately meant for only one individual.

t_ch: 小敌，你的族人…始终在期盼一位新王加冕，是吗？
t_en: De, your people... They have been eagerly awaiting a new king all this time, haven't they?

t_ch: 如果接过神权的是你，他们一定会心甘情愿追随的…因为你是悬锋城的英雄。
t_en: If you take on the divine role, they will have no reservations about following your lead... After all, you're the hero of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 我曾经是。而这，缇里西庇俄丝女士……
t_en: I once was. But Lady Tribios...

t_ch: 这就是我心中<color=#dbc291ff>最深的恐惧</color>。
t_en: Therein lies my <color=#dbc291ff>deepest fear</color>.

t_ch: …晚些再见吧，我会回来的。
t_en: ...We'll talk again later. I'll be back.

t_ch: 小敌。
t_en: De.

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Hm?

t_ch: 无论发生什么，*我们*都会站在你背后的。
t_en: Regardless of what happens, we will always be there for you.

t_ch: …嗯，感谢。
t_en: ...Thank you.

t_ch: 孩子们，别在这里嬉戏打闹。
t_en: You shouldn't run around in here, kids.

t_ch: 呀，是王子！王子也是来泡澡吗？
t_en: Hey, it's the crown prince! Are you also here for a bath?

t_ch: 喔，王子来了！ / 今天真走运！ / 我想听你讲战场上的故事！
t_en: Oh, the prince has arrived! / What a lucky day! / I want to hear your tales from the battlefield!

t_ch: 王子…你们，都是悬锋之子？
t_en: The crown prince...? Are you Kremnoans?

t_ch: 没错，我们都是悬锋人！听长辈说，我家以前就住在竞技场隔壁…的隔壁！
t_en: Yeah, we are! The grown-ups said we used to live next to the arena! ...Well, next to the parts next to it anyway!

t_ch: 我爸爸是蛇妖枪骑兵队的百夫长，妈妈是神盾旅最勇敢的战士，<color=#dbc291ff>克拉特鲁斯爷爷</color>给我家发过好多好多银币呢！
t_en: My dad is a centurion with the Hydra Lancers, and my mom is the bravest warrior in the Godshield Brigade. We used to get tons of silver coins from <color=#dbc291ff>Mr. Krateros</color>!

t_ch: 我父母现在在圣城守备队中服役…他们是悬锋的战士，比所有奥赫玛人都勇敢。
t_en: My parents are serving in the Holy City Garrison now. As warriors of Kremnos, they're braver than any Okheman!

t_ch: 怎么只有你们几个？大人呢？
t_en: Why are you kids here alone? Where are the grown-ups?

t_ch: 嘿，爸爸妈妈都睡着啦，我们是偷偷溜出来的，和克拉特鲁斯爷爷玩捉迷藏！
t_en: Hee-hee, Mom and Dad were asleep, so we snuck out to play hide-and-seek with Mr. Krateros!

t_ch: 这老顽童…我正好有事找他，克拉特鲁斯在何处？
t_en: That old man can be such a child sometimes... Do you know where Krateros is? I happen to be looking for him.

t_ch: 不知道。好一会儿没见他了，不会是找不着我们了吧？
t_en: Sorry, I don't. Come to think of it, we haven't seen him in a while. Did he get lost while trying to find us?

t_ch: 怎么可能，克拉特鲁斯爷爷可是{RUBY_B#国王近身护卫官}王翼冠军{RUBY_E#}，最擅长追踪敌人了。
t_en: No way! Mr. Krateros is a member of the {RUBY_B#King's Guards}King's Aegis{RUBY_E#}. He's the best at hunting down enemies!

t_ch: 他前边在真言狮口那儿数数呢。我好奇想跑过去瞧瞧，但又怕是他设下的陷阱……
t_en: He was counting near Verax Leo when we went to hide. I thought about taking a peek, but who knows if it might be a trap he set?

t_ch: 不错，很警惕。真言狮口，哪一个？
t_en: It was prudent of you to be cautious. So, which Verax Leo was he with?

t_ch: 就在…这里出去，拐几个弯…反正就在浴场附近！
t_en: It's... Um, just take a few turns after you go out from here... It's close to the baths!

t_ch: 我去找他。你们就当游戏已经结束，趁早回家吧。下次别在高温浴池区玩耍了。
t_en: Alright, I'll go look for him. You should consider the game over and head home for now. And don't play around the hot baths again!

t_ch: 王子殿下！
t_en: Wait, Your Highness!

t_ch: 还有什么事？
t_en: Is there something else?

t_ch: 我听人们说，{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的火种已经被您接回来了……
t_en: I heard you retrieved {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} Coreflame...

t_ch: 我们…是不是很快就能回家了？
t_en: Does that mean... we get to go back home soon?

t_ch: 你们几个，可曾去过悬锋城？
t_en: Have you ever been to Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 你们出生在圣城，生活与奥赫玛人无异，对一族的了解也只来自旁人口述。
t_en: You were all born in the holy city and are living lives no different from the Okhemans. Everything you know about our people comes only from the tales of others.

t_ch: 你们甚至没有亲眼见过那柄天上的锋刃…既然如此，何谈返乡？
t_en: You have never even witnessed that blade in the sky with your own eyes... How, then, is a city you have never seen your home?

t_ch: 因为这里不是家。
t_en: Because this place is definitely not home.

t_ch: 爸爸妈妈说，悬锋的孩子打出生起就手持利剑，注定要奔向战场。
t_en: Mom and Dad told me that the children of Kremnos are born to wield swords and fight on the battlefield.

t_ch: 他们还说，王子率领的{RUBY_B#悬锋城流亡军}悬锋孤军{RUBY_E#}是翁法罗斯最勇猛的军队，战无不胜。
t_en: They also said that the {RUBY_B#Kremnoan army in exile}Kremnoan Detachment{RUBY_E#} led by the crown prince is the bravest army across all of Amphoreus. No one can defeat them!

t_ch: 圣城的小孩子不愿和我们一起玩。知道爸爸妈妈是悬锋人，他们就躲得远远的……
t_en: The kids in the holy city don't play with us. The moment they find out that our parents are Kremnoans, they shun us...

t_ch: 但我们不在乎。所有人都知道，总有一天，王子会带我们还乡！
t_en: But we don't care. We all know that the crown prince will take us home one day!

t_ch: 还不是时候。悬锋的年轻人，听我口令：回到父母身边，从今日起，把全部时间用在饮食和锻炼上。想成为合格的悬锋勇士，这两件事决不能落下。
t_en: That time has not yet arrived, but... Young men of Kremnos, heed my command! Return to your parents at once. From hereon, you are to dedicate yourselves to rigorous training and a healthy diet. You must not neglect these two aspects if you are to become a brave warrior of Kremnos.

t_ch: 唯有如此，你们才具备「还乡」的资格。
t_en: Only by following this path can you be deemed worthy of returning home.

t_ch: ——得令，王子殿下！
t_en: Yes, Your Highness!

t_ch: 糟了，是他，那悬锋城的王储……
t_en: Drat! It's the crown prince of Castrum Kremnos...

t_ch: 慌什么！有元老院的指示，黄金裔也不敢对我们怎么样。
t_en: Get a grip! We're here by order of the Council of Elders. Even the Chrysos Heirs have to think twice before doing anything to us.

t_ch: 元老院的走狗，让开。
t_en: Out of my way, lackeys of the Council.
Uh...

t_ch: 奥、奥赫玛的宪兵队正在执行公务，请勿随便靠近！
t_en: Th—The Civil Guard of Okhema is on official duty. Please keep out of this area!

t_ch: 大人，息怒，好好说话……
t_en: Calm down, my lord. Play nice...

t_ch: 别让我说第二遍。
t_en: Don't make me repeat myself.

t_ch: 这次，呃…这次就先放你过去，下、下不为例！
t_en: I—I'll allow you to pass this time... but just this once!

t_ch: 你觉得自己博古通今？那我考考你，你可知道悬锋城的黄金狮首，是怎么被砌到墙上去的？
t_en: So, you think yourself knowledgeable? Then here is a question for you: Do you know how Chryseus Leo in Castrum Kremnos came to be mounted on the wall?

t_ch: 我…我不知道啊，大人，哈哈…怎么上去的？
t_en: I—I don't know, my lord, ahaha... How did it come to be?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，那我就给你说道说道。当年，悬锋城的建立者带着一众战士在特雷托斯城郊围猎雄狮……
t_en: Hahaha! Let me enlighten you. A long time ago, the founder of Castrum Kremnos led a group of warriors to hunt down a lion on the outskirts of the city of Tretos.

t_ch: 那牙尖爪利的恶兽为害多年，巢穴里的骸骨垒得比环绕奥赫玛的山峰还高。
t_en: That vicious beast had been a menace for years, and the bones in its den were stacked even higher than the peaks around Okhema.

t_ch: 可你猜怎么着？那畜生刚一探头，悬锋的战士就立刻冲上前去，电光石火间将它抱摔在地，只一击就劈碎了它的膝盖。然后……
t_en: But do you know what happened? Nary a moment had passed after the beast's head peeked out of its den when the Kremnoan warriors surged forward and brought it down within the blink of an eye, shattering its knees with a single strike! And then—

t_ch: 还没到精彩处呢，你怎么就脸红了？
t_en: ...Why are you red-faced already? We haven't even gotten to the best part yet!

t_ch: 您、是您讲得太生动了，听得我精神焕发！您继续，大人……
t_en: I—It's your storytelling, my lord. You're weaving such a captivating tale that it got my blood pumping. Please, go on...

t_ch: ——哎哟，这不是，万敌阁下嘛？！真是好久不见哪，哈，哈哈……
t_en: — Well, well. If it isn't Lord Mydei! It's been a while, ahaha...

t_ch: 哦？这奥赫玛吹的是什么风……
t_en: Oho, what in Okhema is happening?

t_ch: 居然把<color=#dbc291ff>歌耳戈之子</color>带到我面前了？
t_en: To what do I owe the pleasure, <color=#dbc291ff>son of Gorgo</color>?

t_ch: 克拉特鲁斯…吾师。
t_en: Krateros... My esteemed teacher.

t_ch: 换个地方说话吧，迈德漠斯。跟这石头脑袋交谈，我已经厌烦了。
t_en: Let's talk somewhere else, Mydeimos. I'm tired of speaking with this silly lion.

t_ch: 元老院的眼线，不用管么？
t_en: What about the Council's informants?

t_ch: 两条装模作样的鬣狗，只知道捡食腐肉，压根没有咬人的本事。
t_en: Those two hyenas are nothing but scavengers of rotten meat. They couldn't bite even if they tried.

t_ch: 走吧，让他们跟着也无妨。
t_en: They can follow us if they want to. Let's go.

t_ch: 所以，那黄金狮子到底是怎么上的墙？
t_en: Wait, so how in the world did Chryseus Leo get mounted on the wall!?

t_ch: …怎、怎么停下来了？
t_en: ...Wh—Why have you stopped?

t_ch: 这里不错，适合私下谈话。
t_en: This is a nice spot for a private chat.

t_ch: 动手么，吾师？
t_en: Shall we, sir?

t_ch: 来吧——
t_en: Let's do it!

t_ch: 你、你们想干嘛？！
t_en: Wh—What are you up to!?

t_ch: <size=28>来、快来人啊，救——</size>
t_en: <size=28>S—Someone! Help m—</size>

t_ch: 哼…这下够<color=#dbc291ff>僻静</color>了。
t_en: Hmph... Some <color=#dbc291ff>privacy</color> at last.

t_ch: 说吧，你有何事要找我这老人商量？
t_en: So, tell me, why have you come to seek this old man's counsel?

t_ch: 我想与你讨论…悬锋族人的未来。
t_en: I came to discuss... the future of the Kremnoans.

t_ch: 有趣。我听闻{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}「天谴之矛」{RUBY_E#}的火种已然归位，却并非由你亲手交还。你甚至想将尼卡多利的神力拱手相让——此事当真？
t_en: Interesting. I heard the Coreflame of the Lance of Fury has been restored — though not by your hand — and you're even considering giving up {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} divine power. Is this true?

t_ch: 那些传闻，并非臆造。
t_en: Those rumors are not entirely groundless.

t_ch: 言外之意…你曾想过放弃悬锋的王权，放弃领导你的族人。
t_en: In other words... you did entertain the thought of giving up the throne of Kremnos and forsaking your people.

t_ch: 神力不等同于王权，我族的出路也不止「纷争」一条——
t_en: The divine power is not equivalent to the throne. The path of Strife is also not our people's only way—

t_ch: ——文字游戏！你对悬锋人的境遇心知肚明。
t_en: ...Enough with the word games! You, of all people, should understand the plight of the Kremnoans!

t_ch: 随你一同离开故乡的悬锋孤军，还有他们的后人…支撑我们走到现在的，正是对旧日荣光的渴望。
t_en: Think about all the warriors of the Kremnoan Detachment who left their homeland with you! Think about their children! ...Only the yearning for our former glory days has kept us going all this time.

t_ch: 假如让他们知道，备受族人尊崇的王储萌生了放弃的念头…迈德漠斯，你该如何向你逝去的母亲交代？
t_en: If they ever learn that the crown prince they look up to so much is actually toying with the notion of stepping down... think of the disgrace it would bring upon your departed mother, Mydeimos.

t_ch: 别忘了，她可是为了你——为了重振悬锋的荣光——才会死于{RUBY_B#悬锋先王，万敌之父}欧利庞{RUBY_E#}的毒计！
t_en: You'd best remember that she died for you because of {RUBY_B#Former Kremnoan King, Father of Mydei}Eurypon's{RUBY_E#} schemes! To restore the glory of Kremnos!

t_ch: 既然话已说到这个份上…我问你，是谁杀死了父亲？
t_en: Since you've brought that up... Tell me, who is responsible for my father's death?

t_ch: 你是在羞辱我吗，迈德漠斯？我是老了，但还没老到愚痴。我当然记得，欧利庞死于悬锋孤军的围剿……
t_en: Are you trying to shame me, Mydeimos? I may be old, but I haven't become so decrepit that I've forgotten it all. Of course, I remember that Eurypon died when the Kremnoan Detachment surrounded him.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>是你亲手用长矛贯穿了他的胸膛。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>You drove the spear into his chest with your very own hands.</color>

t_ch: 但那只是结果。
t_en: That was merely the final outcome.

t_ch: 你还没看清吗，吾师？将我的父母，还有悬锋一族送上末路的，正是你我口中的「纷争」，对所谓荣光的追逐，一声声「宁战死，毋荣归」的口号……
t_en: Do you still not see it? What led my parents and our people to their downfall is none other than this Strife we speak of. The quest for glory, the tenet of "Valorous Death Before Glorious Return"...

t_ch: 是每一个悬锋人的执念，无论它正确与否。
t_en: This is the creed of every Kremnoan, no matter whether these beliefs are justified or not.

t_ch: 可笑。能杀死一位王的只有长枪，就连悬锋的婴儿都明白这个道理。
t_en: Don't be ridiculous. The only thing that could slay a king is a spear... Even an infant knows that much.

t_ch: 我曾向你的母亲发誓，要以生命捍卫你的王冠。但我未曾想过，歌耳戈之子会带头质疑…背叛悬锋精神。
t_en: I swore to your mother that I would guard your crown with my life... I never foresaw that the son of Gorgo himself would be the first to turn his back on the Kremnoan spirit.

t_ch: 「歌耳戈」…你的母亲与悬锋城的建立者同名，她的作为也配得上这名字的重量。但你——你若要远离她的荣光，做个逃兵…恕我无法再献上自己的忠诚，迈德漠斯。
t_en: Gorgo... Your mother bore that name, the name of the great founder of Castrum Kremnos, and she upheld that name with honor. If you choose to turn your back on her legacy like a wretched deserter... do not fault me for withholding my loyalty, Mydeimos.

t_ch: 我本想与你共议族人的未来。但…现在似乎不是时候。
t_en: I came to discuss the future of our people with you, but... it appears this is not the right time yet.

t_ch: 走吧，万敌——那些黄金裔是这么称呼你的——走你自以为正确的道路。你骨子里是个我行我素的战士，一旦下定决心，没人能阻止你。
t_en: Go, Mydei... Hah, that's what those Chrysos Heirs call you, isn't it? Go down the path you have deemed correct. Deep down, you have always been one to do as you please, and I am well aware that no one can deter you once you've made up your mind.

t_ch: 可是不要妄图否定悬锋的传统…我们身在奥赫玛，但永远是「纷争」的子民。
t_en: But don't you ever dream of renouncing the traditions of Kremnos... We may reside in Okhema now, but we remain eternally bound to Strife by blood.

t_ch: 在行将毁灭的时代，谈论血统没有意义。
t_en: Bloodlines no longer hold any significance in this era of destruction.

t_ch: 少主，记住：切勿将软肋示人，一头雄狮绝不应混迹于它的猎物之中……
t_en: My young lord, always bear this in mind: Never reveal your vulnerabilities to anyone. A lion should never dwell amidst its prey...

t_ch: 尤其，是它足以主宰整片猎场的时候。
t_en: ...Especially when it wields power enough to dominate the entire hunting ground.

t_ch: 欧利庞，{RUBY_B#万敌}这孩子{RUBY_E#}没做错任何事！
t_en: Eurypon, {RUBY_B#Mydei}the boy{RUBY_E#} did nothing wrong!

t_ch: 这是一场谋杀！别让婴孩无辜的鲜血玷污悬锋人的荣耀——别把软肋暴露给你的敌人！
t_en: This is murder! Do not sully the noble name of Kremnos with the blood of an innocent child! You mustn't reveal your weaknesses to the enemy!

t_ch: 够了！朕心意已决。{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的战魂必须用这孩子的命延续。
t_en: Enough! My decision is final. We must use this child's life to preserve the soul of the great {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 我们…必须拯救悬锋城。
t_en: We must... save Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 荒谬！他的手就连枪头都举不起来，你觉得他能威胁到悬锋城？
t_en: Don't be absurd! Those tiny hands lack the strength to even lift a spear! Do you truly believe him a threat to Castrum Kremnos!?

t_ch: 你要是真这么做，那才是断绝了悬锋的血脉——让我们千百年的荣光变成彻头彻尾的笑柄！
t_en: You'll be ending the bloodline of Kremnos if you do this. Our millennia-old glory will become nothing more than a joke!

t_ch: 你忘了，悬锋人不相信血脉。我们的王只能从{RUBY_B#悬锋祭典}浴血的角斗{RUBY_E#}中诞生……
t_en: Have you forgotten that we Kremnoans place no value in bloodlines? It is through {RUBY_B#Kremnos Festival}pure bloodshed and slaughter{RUBY_E#} that the kings of Kremnos have ascended to the throne...

t_ch: 至于悬锋城千百年的「荣光」，那本来就是个笑话——杀戮就是杀戮，为虚荣而高兴的杀人凶手，并不比翁法罗斯最凶蛮的野兽更高贵。
t_en: As for our millennia-old "glory"... it has always been a joke in my eyes. Glorify it all you want, but it will not alter the nature of slaughter. Those who revel in it are but murderers. They are no more noble than the most monstrous beasts in Amphoreus.

t_ch: 大言不惭！你，还有悬锋诸王，你们的每一顶王冠，哪个不是从父辈的尸身摘得……
t_en: Enough with your words! Among all the kings of Kremnos, which one did not inherit the crown only after plucking it from the lifeless body of his father?

t_ch: 可现在呢？难道你想用短短几句话，洗清自己染血的双手么？
t_en: But listen to yourself now! Do you truly believe you can cleanse the blood from your hands through mere words alone?

t_ch: 不，恰恰相反：我正要结束这血腥的循环……
t_en: Nay, quite the contrary. I will end this cycle of bloodshed...

t_ch: 就从这孩子开始——就在我染血的双手中结束。
t_en: ...Starting with this child. With these bloodstained hands, I will end it all.

t_ch: 你做不到，欧利庞！你只是个懦夫，一个只敢将屠刀挥向自己骨肉的卑鄙小人，弑亲禽兽……
t_en: Your failure is inevitable, Eurypon! You are just a coward who can only raise his blade against his own child! A beast that murders his own kin!

t_ch: 王翼冠军们、督政官们，你们不能袖手旁观！如果你们还是光荣的{RUBY_B#悬锋建城者}歌耳戈{RUBY_E#}之子，就提枪跟上我，结束这场血腥的闹剧！
t_en: King's Aegis and Ephors of Kremnos, you cannot sit idly by and watch this atrocity unfold! If you consider yourselves the honorable sons and daughters of {RUBY_B#Kremnos Founder}Gorgo{RUBY_E#}, then take up your spears and join me in putting an end to this bloody charade!

t_ch: 可还有人…提出异议？若有，就拿上铜枪，上前来证明自己！
t_en: ...Does anyone else have any objections? If so, come forward with your spear and prove your mettle!
...Understood.
Five...
Four...
Three...

t_ch: 没有人敢随我来么？你们这些懦夫，那王冠竟让你们变得如此软弱？
t_en: No one dares stand by my side? Cowards! Has that crown stripped you of your courage!?
Two...

t_ch: 一。
t_en: One.

t_ch: 看来无人提出异议。基于悬锋元老院「一致沉默」之原则，此事已无复议必要。
t_en: It appears there are no objections. Then, in accordance with the Kremnoan Council's principle of tacit consent, the decision is final.

t_ch: 在列位公民见证下，此子将沉入冥海：以飨{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}「天谴之矛」{RUBY_E#}，并与天地间众英灵。
t_en: With all present as witnesses, this child shall descend into the Sea of Souls, where he will nourish the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#} and join the fallen heroes.

t_ch: 愿命运与尊神恭候你……
t_en: May fate and the gods await your arrival...

t_ch: 吾儿…迈德漠斯。
t_en: My son... Mydeimos.

t_ch: 住手！
t_en: Stop!

t_ch: 说吧。看在你我同出生、共入死的份上…朕给你最后一次机会。
t_en: If you have more to say, then speak. On account of the life we have shared together, I grant you this one final chance.

t_ch: 「天谴之锋」在上，并由元老院诸将士共作见证：我，歌耳戈，依悬锋法典发起「挑战王权」之动议——
t_en: With the Blade of Fury above us and the Council bearing witness, I, Gorgo, hereby move to challenge the crown in accordance with the laws of Kremnos!

t_ch: 失格的王啊，以元老院之名，我勒令你与我角斗！
t_en: O unworthy king, in the name of the Council of Elders, I demand that you duel me!

t_ch: 呵…也好。
t_en: Heh... Very well.

t_ch: 那就来看看…悬锋将落向谁的头颅吧。
t_en: We shall see who the Kremnoan blade strikes down.

t_ch: 迈德漠斯，吾儿啊……
t_en: Mydeimos, my son...

t_ch: 他们总劝我遗忘…可我岂能忘记？
t_en: They all tell me to forget... But how could I ever?

t_ch: {RUBY_B#悬锋建城者}歌耳戈{RUBY_E#}之子，浴血代冠……
t_en: The son of {RUBY_B#Kremnos Founder}Gorgo{RUBY_E#}... will be crowned in blood.

t_ch: 若无王冠便无以为悬锋…那我就夺来它，掷碎它，激醒众人吧。
t_en: If there is no Kremnos without the crown... then I shall seize the crown and smash it to pieces to bring the people to their senses.

t_ch: 母亲怒号。
t_en: The mother roared in anger.

t_ch: 众军沉默不语。
t_en: The armies remained in silence.

t_ch: 唯有啼哭与昏黑的冥海涛声回荡。
t_en: Only the cries and the waves of the Sea of Souls echoed.

t_ch: 哈哈…鲜血的味道…
t_en: Haha... the sweet taste of blood...

t_ch: 去给「纷争」做祭品吧！
t_en: Offer yourself to Strife!

t_ch: 「纷争」站在我这边！
t_en: Strife is on my side!

t_ch: 死吧，懦夫！
t_en: Perish, you coward!

t_ch: 你这…嗜血的蛮族…
t_en: You... barbaric bloodseeker...

t_ch: 谁是下一个？
t_en: Who's next?
No...

t_ch: 刻法勒在上啊……
t_en: By Kephale above...

t_ch: 去给你们的疯王陪葬吧！
t_en: Join your Mad King in death!

t_ch: 救世主…你在哪里？
t_en: Deliverer... where have you gone?

t_ch: 为了奥赫玛！死吧！
t_en: In Okhema's name! Perish!

t_ch: 处决！处决！
t_en: Execute! Execute!

t_ch: 干掉他！
t_en: Kill him!

t_ch: 你们和我没什么两样…
t_en: You lot are just like me...

t_ch: 为了救世主！
t_en: For the Deliverer!

t_ch: 动手！动手！
t_en: Attack! Attack!

t_ch: 一段时间之前 云石天宫内
t_en: Some time before, in the Marmoreal Palace...

t_ch: 缇安…？
t_en: Trianne...?

t_ch: 怎么了…是树庭那面遇到麻烦了么？
t_en: What's wrong...? Is there trouble at the Grove?

t_ch: 是。有一道感应转瞬即逝，但特别强烈…{M#他们}{F#她们}似乎遇见了棘手的麻烦。
t_en: Yes, I sensed something... It was fleeting, but very strong... It feels like they're in some big trouble.

t_ch: …缇安又使用了「百界门」。
t_en: ...Trianne used the Century Gate again.

t_ch: 能看见具体的画面吗？
t_en: Can you see what's actually happening?

t_ch: 不行，和平时一样模糊。但敌人的气息消失了，也许{M#他们}{F#她们}转移到了安全的地方吧…大概。
t_en: No. It's fuzzy like always, but the enemy's aura has disappeared. Maybe... they've moved to a safe place...?

t_ch: 但愿只是虚惊一场。相信{NICKNAME}和遐蝶吧，吾师……
t_en: Let's hope that it's just a false alarm. We should place our trust in {NICKNAME} and Castorice, teacher...

t_ch: {M#他们}{F#她们}会保护好缇安的。
t_en: ...They will keep Trianne safe.

t_ch: 你怎么把*我们*当小孩子啦，阿雅。
t_en: Agy, you're talking like we are little kids.

t_ch: 总有一天，缇安会走到极限，缇宝会从她手里接过「门匠」的职责，这事*我们*心知肚明。
t_en: One day, Trianne will reach her limit, and Tribbie will take on the role of gatekeeper from her — we are all aware of that.

t_ch: *我*只是…有些担心她的心智。出发前，缇安已经连短句都念不清了……
t_en: I am just... a little concerned about her state of mind. Trianne was already unable to speak even in short sentences when they left...

t_ch: 至少让*我们*好好道个别吧。
t_en: We want to say a proper goodbye at the very least.

t_ch: …我会和你一起祈祷的。
t_en: ...I will pray with you.

t_ch: 谢谢。阿雅，这次得麻烦你陪*我们*去趟浴场，放松一下啦……
t_en: Thanks. And Agy, it looks like we will need you to come with us to the baths this time to unwind...

t_ch: 感觉，我俩都很久没有笑过了。
t_en: It feels like neither of us have laughed for a long while.
Ugh...

t_ch: 这、这是怎么了？！
t_en: Wh—What happened here?

t_ch: 小…心……
t_en: Watch... out...

t_ch: 小心…悬锋人……
t_en: Watch out... for the Kremnoan...

t_ch: 悬锋人？这片惨状…是他们造成的？
t_en: Kremnoan? This is... their doing?

t_ch: 阿雅，先别管这些了，快救救伤员！
t_en: Agy, now's not the time to dwell on that. Let's help the injured people first!

t_ch: ——各位，请让一让！
t_en: ...Excuse me! Coming through!

t_ch: 二位！你们也在，那我就放心了。
t_en: Oh, you two are here as well! That's a relief.

t_ch: 怎么昏光庭院也来了！到底出了什么事？
t_en: Even the Twilight Courtyard is involved? What in the world happened here?

t_ch: 其实我只是来放松的…只是刚到附近，就见好多人横七竖八倒在地上。
t_en: I actually just came to relax... But as soon as I got here, I saw all these people lying on the ground.

t_ch: 我检查了一番，他们没有大碍，感觉只是…水温太高，泡得太久。
t_en: I've examined them, and they don't seem to be seriously ill. It feels like... they've just spent too much time in the hot water.

t_ch: 「好多人」同时因为池水高温昏迷？浴场的管理人在何处？
t_en: Multiple individuals fainting simultaneously from the hot pool's steam? Where is the manager of the baths?

t_ch: 他消失了，不知去向。有些人嘴里念念有词，说是「悬锋人」干的…阿格莱雅女士有头绪吗？
t_en: He disappeared, and no one knows where he's gone to. Some people have been saying that the Kremnoans are behind this... Lady Aglaea, what do you think about this?

t_ch: 让这些人好好休息吧。两位，随我来。
t_en: Let these people rest. The two of you, please come with me.

t_ch: 阿雅，你想到什么了吗？
t_en: Have you thought of something, Agy?

t_ch: 当然。虽然不知道他们究竟在打什么算盘，但是时候结束这场闹剧了。
t_en: Yes. While their intentions elude me, it's time to bring this farce to a close.

t_ch: 小白！连小白也……
t_en: Snowy! Even Snowy...

t_ch: 凶…手……
t_en: The... culprit...

t_ch: 凶手…是…迈德漠斯……
t_en: The culprit... is... Mydeimos...
...Ugh!

t_ch: 小敌！你把大家怎么了？！
t_en: De! What did you do to everybody!?

t_ch: 哼…不过是在又一场角斗中胜出了而已。
t_en: Tch... It's just another duel where I claimed victory.

t_ch: 角、角斗？
t_en: D—Duel?

t_ch: …果然如此。
t_en: ...As I expected.

t_ch: 这二人，表面上是圣城最强大的战士，但终究掩盖不了少年心性……
t_en: On the surface, these two are the mightiest warriors in the holy city, but they're still impulsive youths at heart...

t_ch: 说说吧，万敌？告诉我们，你和白厄做了些什么傻事。
t_en: Speak up, Mydei. Tell us exactly what kind of foolishness you and Phainon indulged in this time.

t_ch: 一段时间以前……
t_en: Some time ago...

t_ch: （恢复得如何了，朋友？）
t_en: (How's your recovery coming along, my friend?)

t_ch: （失败乃兵家常事，别在意。）
t_en: (Winning and losing are all part of a warrior's life. Don't let it get to you.)

t_ch: （泡澡放松一下吧，兄弟。）
t_en: (Why don't you take a bath to recharge, buddy?)

t_ch: （不，不太对。）
t_en: (No. None of those sound right.)

t_ch: （振作，你还有更远的目标。）
t_en: (Keep your chin up. You have greater things to achieve.)

t_ch: （来喝点么，伙计？）
t_en: (Want to grab a drink with me, pal?)

t_ch: （不介意的话，我们聊聊。）
t_en: (If you're up for it, let's have a talk.)
(...Forget it.)

t_ch: 你还活着啊，「救世主」？
t_en: I see you're still alive and kicking, Deliverer.

t_ch: …我没看走眼吧？了不起的「救世主」，终于愿意出门见光了？
t_en: ...Am I seeing things? Is the magnificent Deliverer finally deigning to step outside?

t_ch: 哼。
t_en: Hmph.

t_ch: 「哼」…这算哪门子回答？
t_en: "Hmph"...? What kind of response is that?

t_ch: 没什么——模仿你平时的样子罢了。
t_en: Nothing. I was just imitating your usual response.

t_ch: 我没在你身上感受到特别的气息…半神的考验，你通过了么？
t_en: I don't feel anything special from you... Have you passed the demigod trial?

t_ch: 仪式还未重启。免得你忘记——距离我将你揪出试炼才没过多久。
t_en: The ritual hasn't reopened yet. In case you forgot — It hasn't been THAT long since I pulled you out of the trial.

t_ch: 我没忘。
t_en: I didn't forget.

t_ch: 不用谢。
t_en: Then, you're welcome.

t_ch: 「…长夜最后的光亮，城垣坚固的奥赫玛。」
t_en: "In twilight's last gleam, Okhema stands bold. Her walls sturdy, tales untold."

t_ch: 「有多少人远道而来，以为她是常有无花果和橄榄的丰美园林……」
t_en: "Dreaming of her fruitful gardens, pilgrims arrive from distant lands. Figs and olives, O so grand..."

t_ch: 「…却不曾想，跌入了燃烧的炉膛？」
t_en: "...Yet little do they know, that they will step into flames, fuel to the fire, a burning brand."

t_ch: 你又在耍什么把戏？
t_en: What are you playing at this time?

t_ch: 一时来了兴致，想念几句诗而已。悬锋的王子，不会听不懂诗歌吧？
t_en: I just felt like reciting a few lines of poetry. Surely the crown prince of Kremnos knows how to appreciate poetry, right?

t_ch: 悬锋人不读这种软弱的文字，但{RUBY_B#歌耳巴尼帕耳大图书馆}我的图书馆{RUBY_E#}里不乏这类藏书——
t_en: Kremnoans steer clear of such weak literature, but {RUBY_B#Garbaniphoro Grand Library}my library{RUBY_E#} doesn't lack collections of this kind —

t_ch: 这几句表达了作者的思乡之情，正如奥赫玛离大部分人的故乡都很遥远。
t_en: These lines express the poet's yearning for their home, just as Okhema is distant from many people's homes.

t_ch: ——我听得懂诗，{RUBY_B#混账}HKS{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: ...I know how to interpret poetry, {RUBY_B#scoundrel}HKS{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 哈…别装深沉了，朋友。感谢你在危机关头拉了我一把，但依我看，你也没做好试炼的准备吧？
t_en: Ha... There's no point in pretending to be profound, my friend. Thank you for helping me out when I was in a tight spot, but it seems to me that you aren't prepared for the trial either.

t_ch: 别把你我的处境混为一谈。我背后还有族人，在解决他们的问题前，我不能独自登神。
t_en: Don't compare your situation with mine. My people are still relying on me. I can't ascend to godhood on my own without first resolving their issues.

t_ch: 嚯…听你的意思，勇猛的迈德漠斯，悬锋王位的继承人，居然想打破一族延续千年的古老传统？
t_en: So... are you suggesting that you, the mighty Mydeimos and the heir to the throne of Kremnos, wish to defy the tradition that has endured among your people for millennia?

t_ch: 该怎么做，我仍在思考。
t_en: I'm still thinking about what I should do.

t_ch: 算了，恐怕还轮不到我来给你提建议。
t_en: Never mind. It's probably not my place to offer you counsel.

t_ch: 不过，我倒是突然冒出一个想法……
t_en: An idea does strike me, though...

t_ch: 讲吧，又是什么馊主意？
t_en: Go ahead. Let's see what terrible ideas you've come up with this time.

t_ch: 或许我们都需要释放压力。来吧，迈德漠斯，延续你我之前的竞赛……
t_en: Maybe we both need to relieve some stress. Come, Mydeimos! Let's recommence our previous competition...

t_ch: 这次——就比比谁能在*超高温*浴池里坚持更久！
t_en: This time... We'll see who can stay in the super hot bath for longer!

t_ch: 怎么，怯场了？
t_en: What? Are you chickening out?

t_ch: 悬锋人的字典里没有「怯场」二字。我只是觉得可笑。野史学家，你难道没听说过悬锋人沐浴，用的都是锻剑淬铁的滚烫铁水？
t_en: The phrase "chickening out" does not exist in the Kremnoan language. I just find the situation absurd, that's all. All-knowing historian, are you not aware that we Kremnoans use boiling water from sword forging for our baths?

t_ch: 今日，我就来教你这些宫廷礼数！
t_en: Looks like I'll have to teach you some royal etiquette today!

t_ch: 于是！一场旷古绝伦的桑拿大战开始了。
t_en: And thus, an unprecedented sauna battle commences.

t_ch: 那二人的决心登峰造极，强烈的意志令水汽都为之色变，气焰裹挟着水汽直冲云霄，遮天蔽日，使人目不能视……
t_en: With unparalleled resolve, the two warriors' determination even alters the very hue of the steam. Flames intertwine with scalding vapor, reaching toward the heavens, shrouding the sky and the sun. The spectacle is so magnificent that none dare gaze upon it directly...

t_ch: 既然看不清现场赛况…闲着也是闲着，不如来听我讲一段与「王」有关的故事吧？
t_en: Since we can't see what's happening in the competition... Why don't I regale you with a tale of "kings" to pass the time?

t_ch: 还记得某位老战士提及的，「歌耳戈猎狮」的传说吗？故事的主人…主狮公，其实并非一方之霸，还有一头剑羚能与之分庭抗礼。
t_en: Do you recall the legend of the "Gorgo Lion" mentioned by a certain veteran? The central figure — the "mane" character — was not the sole sovereign of that land. An oryx stood as its formidable rival.

t_ch: 早在黄金世前，两头野兽便已在特雷托斯平原上捉对厮杀。雄狮当剑羚作盘中餐，而剑羚视之为一堵蔑视、压迫、磨难的高墙，它誓用犄角将其推翻……
t_en: Long before the dawn of Era Chrysea, the two beasts started their timeless battle on the plains of Tretos. The lion regarded the oryx as its food, while the oryx perceived the lion as a scornful, oppressive, and challenging barrier that it swore to topple with its antlers...

t_ch: 于是，数万年如一日地，两头野兽在荒野中开始了角逐和追逃——
t_en: For tens of thousands of years, without respite, the two creatures thus continued their relentless pursuit and rivalry...

t_ch: 它瞠乎其后。
t_en: It fixated on its rival's fleeing form in the chase.

t_ch: 它望其项背。
t_en: It set its sights on the other's neck, nearly within reach.

t_ch: 它无路可退。
t_en: It was stuck in a corner.

t_ch: 它插翅难飞。
t_en: It had nowhere to escape.

t_ch: 翁法罗斯的大地上，有些生命死于黄昏，有些生命死于拂晓，而有些生命——它们命中注定死于正午的烈阳！
t_en: On the land of Amphoreus, some lives meet their end at dusk, some at dawn, and yet others... are fated to perish under the blistering sun at noon!

t_ch: 终于！到了一日中最为炽烈的时刻，强有力的命运应召而至——
t_en: And at last, the most scorching moment of the day descended upon them! The formidable hand of fate heeded their call and arrived...

t_ch: 两位阁下，需要我把温度调低些么？
t_en: My lords, would you like me to reduce the temperature a little?
Hmph...

t_ch: 怎、怎么？呵…你怂了？
t_en: Wh—What? Heh... You're chickening out already?
...Get lost.

t_ch: 您，您是在让我滚，还是……
t_en: A—Are you asking me to get lost? Or...

t_ch: 你自己…看着办……
t_en: Do... whatever... you want...

t_ch: …那就恕我僭越了，阁下。
t_en: ...Please forgive my presumptuousness, my lords.

t_ch: 还真是…丑态百出啊。
t_en: ...What a spectacle of folly.

t_ch: 不自量力地向悬锋人发出挑战，这就是下场。
t_en: This is what you get for daring to challenge a Kremnoan without understanding your own place.

t_ch: 我是说…你们的小小游戏，可是连带着让不少人一起遭了殃呢。
t_en: What I'm saying is... Your little game dragged down quite a few innocent souls alongside you.

t_ch: …那些被杀气震晕的浴客，我们会送他们离开的。
t_en: ...We will personally escort back home the customers left awestruck by our formidable presence.

t_ch: 这…不公平……
t_en: This... This isn't fair...

t_ch: 你穿得…比我…清凉多了……
t_en: You are wearing... way less... than I am...

t_ch: …全副武装扎进高温浴池，这是什么新风尚吗？
t_en: ...Is this the latest trend now? Getting into the baths while dressed in full armor?

t_ch: 别用那眼神盯着我…是他下的战书。悬锋的字典里没有「逃避」二字。
t_en: Don't look at me like that... It was he who challenged me. There is no word for "flee" in the Kremnoan language.

t_ch: 好了，这场闹剧就到此为止吧？勇士们，该动起来了。
t_en: Very well, let us bring this farce to a close, shall we? Warriors, it is time to get moving.

t_ch: 状态恢复后，白厄与万敌将晕倒的浴客们送离了案发现场……
t_en: After recovery, Phainon and Mydei escorted the fainted bathers away from the scene.

t_ch: 这一次的护送较量，白厄以<unbreak>27</unbreak>比<unbreak>25</unbreak>的惊人优势险胜。
t_en: This time, Phainon narrowly triumphed with a stunning <unbreak>27</unbreak> to <unbreak>25</unbreak> victory in terms of the number of trips safely escorted.

t_ch: 呵…逐火之旅，真是道阻且长啊。
t_en: Alas... The Flame-Chase Journey is destined to be a lengthy one, replete with obstacles.

t_ch: …不过，多亏了他俩，悲伤的气氛都烟消云散了。
t_en: ...But thanks to those two, all the gloom has vanished into thin air.

t_ch: 虽然刚经历一场闹剧，但云石天宫难得这么清净，不如继续先前的行程吧。小风堇，你也一起来吗？
t_en: Though we just had quite the commotion, it's not every day that we find the Marmoreal Palace this serene. Shall we continue with our previous plans? Hyacine, would you care to join us?

t_ch: 这么空旷的浴场是难得一见……
t_en: Such an empty bath is indeed a rare sight...

t_ch: 我本意也想在术后放松一下，那就承蒙缇宝女士邀请啦！
t_en: I was looking forward to unwinding after work. I'd be delighted to accept your invitation, Lady Tribbie!

t_ch: 让我先试试…水温刚好呢！
t_en: Let me check... The water temperature feels just perfect!

t_ch: 如何，这就是享用整座浴场的滋味。
t_en: So, how is the feeling of having the entire bath to yourselves?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，太舒服啦……
t_en: Heehee, it's sooo good...

t_ch: 不过，缇宝老师…你不一起泡泡么？
t_en: But, Lady Tribbie... Aren't you going to soak in the bath with us?

t_ch: *我们*就不啦，毕竟变成小孩子了嘛。
t_en: We will have to pass. After all, we've become children.

t_ch: 请见谅。受{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的{RUBY_B#诅咒}赐福{RUBY_E#}影响，她已不能耐受这池子的水温了。
t_en: Please understand that she can no longer endure the heat of this pool due to the blessings of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}.
Oh...

t_ch: 你是对*我们*的事感到好奇吗，小风堇？
t_en: Are you curious about us, Hyacine?

t_ch: 是有些好奇啦，但直觉告诉我…是不是换个话题比较好？
t_en: I am, but something tells me... that it might be better to change the topic?

t_ch: 紧张什么呀，不是不能说的事！其实呢，*我们*在很久很久以前…也像阿雅一样，是<color=#dbc291ff>成熟的大人</color>哦。
t_en: Don't get all nervous. It's not something we can't discuss! The truth is... a long, long time ago... we were a mature adult just like Agy.

t_ch: 但因为种种原因，*我们*接过{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的神职，得到了「门径」的力量……
t_en: Then, because of reasons... we inherited {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}' divinity and gained the power of "Passage"...

t_ch: 而作为交换，付出的便是<color=#dbc291ff>灵魂分裂</color>的代价。
t_en: And in return, the price we had to pay was the splitting of our soul.

t_ch: 每一次洞开「百界门」，都会深化分裂对缇宝她们的影响。从肉体到心智，一步步向着婴儿退行。
t_en: Each opening of the Century Gate intensifies the strain on Tribbie and the others. They are gradually regressing towards infancy, both in body and mind.

t_ch: 其实*我们*也不知道终点会是什么样子。总而言之，这就是*我们*变成小孩子的原因。
t_en: We don't know what awaits us at the end, either. But anyway, that's why we have turned into children.

t_ch: 那…先前和{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}对决时，你们在奥赫玛上空张开的巨大「百界门」，岂不是……
t_en: In that case... when you opened that giant Century Gate above Okhema during the battle against {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 没办法，逐火之旅注定不会一帆风顺嘛。
t_en: Well, there's no way around it. We all know that the Flame-Chase Journey won't be a smooth ride.

t_ch: 也许…会有办法的。智种学派的核心课题就是灵魂，针对它的损耗，或是弥补已经消散的灵魂…只要研究继续下去，没准会有新的发现。
t_en: Maybe... We can find a way to fix it. The primary research focus of Nousporists is the soul. We explore its depletion and methods to restore dispersed souls... As long as research continues, there might be a breakthrough that can help.

t_ch: 我们会尽力而为。
t_en: We will do everything we can.

t_ch: 第一反应竟然是否定么？不愧是那刻夏的门生。
t_en: So, your first reaction is in fact denial? You're indeed Anaxa's student.

t_ch: 我期待你的好消息，风堇。
t_en: I look forward to your good news, Hyacine.

t_ch: 哈哈，这样又多了一个盼着明天的理由呢……
t_en: Haha, now there's another reason to look forward to tomorrow...
...Ugh!

t_ch: 缇宝女士？！怎、怎么了？
t_en: Lady Tribbie!? Wh—What's wrong?

t_ch: 好像…不太对劲，强烈的感应又来了…是缇安？
t_en: Something's... off. It's that strong feeling again... Is it Trianne?

t_ch: 不、不对…这次不是缇安……
t_en: Wait, no... It's not Trianne this time...

t_ch: …是缇宁！缇宁遇上危险了…！
t_en: ...It's Trinnon! Trinnon is in danger...!

t_ch: 与此同时……
t_en: In the meantime...

t_ch: 哈，这次护送浴客是我赢了。
t_en: Ha, I win this round of escorting the bathers home.

t_ch: 单方面开启的竞赛，如何能够作数？
t_en: How does one contender alone make a valid competition?

t_ch: 看看你，说两句恭维话又不会掉层皮。
t_en: Offering a compliment or two wouldn't be the end of you, you know.

t_ch: …{RUBY_B#混账}HKS{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: ...{RUBY_B#scoundrel}HKS{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 海乙…你在说什么，家乡话？
t_en: Hai... What is that word? Is it your native tongue?

t_ch: 「鬣狗」——我在将你比作一条瘦弱的鬣狗。顺带一提，你的重音完全不对。
t_en: It means "hyena" — I'm likening you to a weak hyena. By the way, you emphasized the completely wrong syllable.

t_ch: 咱俩认识也有段时间了，听你说悬锋古语的次数…屈指可数啊。
t_en: Though we've been acquainted for some time, I have... rarely heard you talk in the ancient tongue of Kremnos.

t_ch: 因为我也不擅长这门语言。听和读不成问题。但若要让我以古语写作……
t_en: It's because I'm not exactly a master at it, either. I can listen and read just fine, but when it comes to writing...

t_ch: 你还会写东西？
t_en: You can write?

t_ch: 你的发言仿佛没有脑子。
t_en: You speak as if you don't have a brain.

t_ch: 哈哈，开玩笑的。也难怪，毕竟你童年一直在…漂泊，离家很远。
t_en: Haha, that was in jest. I understand. After all, you... spent all your childhood wandering far from home.

t_ch: 多关心下你自己吧，救世主。除去自报家门的场合，你也从不提及故乡。
t_en: Worry about yourself first, Deliverer. You also never mention your hometown apart from when you need to introduce yourself.

t_ch: 呵…因为实在没什么可说的。哀丽秘榭只是个小村庄，因为战火，早就不复存在了。
t_en: Well... It's because there isn't much to say. Aedes Elysiae is simply a tiny village that disappeared many years ago in the flames of war.

t_ch: 我唯一能见到它的场合就是在梦中。还有……
t_en: The only times I get to see it are in my dreams... and in...

t_ch: 还有？
t_en: And in?

t_ch: …在{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}「天谴之矛」{RUBY_E#}的试炼里。
t_en: ...And in the trial from the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 果然啊。
t_en: I knew it.

t_ch: 嗯，我看见了，燃烧的哀丽秘榭，我的亲人、朋友、同族…所有人都倒在火海里。
t_en: Yes, I saw it all. Aedes Elysiae ablaze, with everyone — my family, friends, and my kin — lying in a sea of flames.

t_ch: 天上挂着半轮血日，就和那时一样，那个凶手在我面前……
t_en: A blood-red half-sun hung in the sky, just like on that day. And before my eyes, that murderer...

t_ch: …杀死了<color=#dbc291ff>昔涟</color>。
t_en: ...killed <color=#dbc291ff>Cyrene</color>.

t_ch: 不过这一次，我看清了它的样貌：<color=#dbc291ff>黑色的斗篷，诡异的面具。破碎的、散发着不详气息的巨剑……</color>
t_en: But I saw its form this time... <color=#dbc291ff>A black cloak, an eerie mask, and a broken giant sword that carried an ominous aura...</color>

t_ch: 在试炼里，我和那家伙交手了。但过去这么久，即便我带着决心一路战斗、成长、变强…我还是战胜不了它。
t_en: I fought it in the trial. But even though I've been fighting, improving, and resolutely getting stronger all this time... I still couldn't defeat it.

t_ch: 仇恨蒙蔽了你的双眼，你差点就要迷失在那试炼里了。
t_en: You were blinded by hatred and almost lost yourself in that trial.

t_ch: 对…所以我由衷地感谢你，迈德漠斯。
t_en: That's right... And that's why I am truly thankful to you, Mydeimos.

t_ch: 哼，总算学会礼仪了，倒也不算太晚。
t_en: Hmph, you've finally picked up some manners. Better late than never.

t_ch: 那你呢，万敌？你在那试炼里看见了什么…你的恐惧又是什么？
t_en: Then what about you, Mydei? What did you see in that trial? ...What do you fear?

t_ch: 首先，悬锋人的字典里没有「恐惧」二字。
t_en: There is no word for "fear" in the Kremnoan language.

t_ch: …你们的字典里是不是压根没字？
t_en: ...I'm getting the feeling that your language has no words at all.

t_ch: 然后，我在其中看到了奥赫玛。以及……
t_en: But to answer your question, I saw Okhema in the trial, as well as...

t_ch: …我曾经的战友们。
t_en: ...My old comrades.

t_ch: 赫菲斯辛、帕狄卡斯、莱昂、托勒密，还有朴塞塔…自我从冥海归来，那五人便始终拱卫左右。
t_en: Hephaestion, Perdikkas, Leonnius, Ptolemy, and Peucesta... Ever since I returned from the Sea of Souls, these five had been by my side.

t_ch: 流亡的岁月，他们与我同生共死。那十年时光，颠沛流离，但也值得怀念。每个夜晚，我们都会在营火边围坐，畅饮蜜酿，纵情高歌……
t_en: We faced life and death together during our days in exile. Those ten years were arduous, yet filled with moments worth treasuring. Each night, we would gather by the campfire, savoring honey brews and singing our hearts out...

t_ch: 翁法罗斯的野风有铁锈的味道。赫菲斯辛总笑话我喝蜜酿加羊奶，说把鲜红的血色拌得不伦不类。他生得瘦小，但在战场上凶悍无比，杀敌如麻。
t_en: The wind in the wilds of Amphoreus smells like rust. Hephaestion would always tease me about adding goat milk to the honey brew, claiming I ruined the blood-red color of the drink. He had a scrawny figure, but was a relentless force on the battlefield, dispatching foes without so much as a blink.

t_ch: 帕狄卡斯精通制药，净整些偏方怪方；莱昂善于奔跑，是最受信赖的信使。
t_en: Perdikkas was an expert in concocting medicines, delving into all those strange folk remedies. Leonnius was a swift runner and our most reliable messenger.

t_ch: 托勒密总爱咬文嚼字，离开悬锋时，从大图书馆顺走了不少古籍——都是我家的，也不用还了。至于朴塞塔…呵，他不爱说话，但弹得一手好琴……
t_en: Ptolemy was a real bookworm, always spouting off in chapters and verses. When we left Kremnos, he made off with a stack of ancient tomes from the grand library — not like it mattered, since my family owns the library. And then there was Peucesta... Heh, he was the silent type, but a truly masterful musician...

t_ch: 这五位…我还是第一次听说他们的名字。
t_en: ...This is the first time I've heard you mention these five and their names.

t_ch: 当然。在悬锋孤军与奥赫玛联合前，他们就战死了。
t_en: They died on the battlefield before the Kremnoan Detachment managed to join Okhema.

t_ch: 先是帕狄卡斯，他死在第二个{RUBY_B#四月}耕耘月{RUBY_E#}，被拉冬人的毒箭射中腹部。他的药方能救所有人，唯独没救下自己。
t_en: Perdikkas was the first to go. He was shot in the stomach by a poisonous arrow from the people of Ladon in the second {RUBY_B#Fourth Month}Month of Cultivation{RUBY_E#}. In the end, his remedies mended all but himself.

t_ch: 「迈德漠斯，不要悲伤。我已挣得荣耀，用光荣的死亡回敬夙敌……」
t_en: "Mydeimos, do not mourn. I have achieved honor by bestowing a noble death upon the enemy..."

t_ch: 莱昂死在第五个冬天，哀地里亚的暴风雪持续了整整三个月。风雪最大的那夜，敌人发动了袭击。
t_en: Leonnius died in the fifth winter. Aidonia's snowstorm lasted three full months. On the night of the fiercest storm, our foes launched their assault.

t_ch: 哀地里亚…是遐蝶以前所在的城邦？
t_en: Aidonia... Is that the city Castorice used to live in?

t_ch: 就是那群狂信徒，他们如悬锋人一般视死如生，唯独少了对荣誉的敬畏。
t_en: Yes. Those zealots were as fearless as Kremnoans in the face of death, but they lacked respect for honor.

t_ch: 他们在无底沼泽中围追堵截，莱昂为了殿后被斩断双腿。他消失在异乡的泥土里，尸骨无存。
t_en: They cornered us in a bottomless swamp. Leonnius covered for our retreat, and they cut his legs off. He vanished into the mire of a foreign land, his body never to be recovered.

t_ch: 「多好啊，迈德漠斯…我终于能将你成王的讯息带向冥界了……」
t_en: "This is perfect, Mydeimos... I can finally deliver the news of you ascending the throne to the nether realm..."

t_ch: 那之后的第二十个黄昏，我们终于反攻入哀地里亚城中，接受了僭主的求和，但负隅顽抗的叛军却在城中设伏……
t_en: Twenty dusks later, we finally breached the walls of Aidonia and entertained the tyrant's plea for peace. However, the rebels refused to surrender and set up an ambush within the city...

t_ch: 托勒密和他的骑兵队，还未来得及品尝胜利便全军覆没。燃烧崩塌的方尖碑林成为了他们的坟丘。
t_en: Ptolemy and his cavalry met their end before they could even relish our triumph. Those obelisks that collapsed after being set on fire became their final resting place.

t_ch: 「迈德漠斯，请你转告我的家人：打倒我托勒密的并非歹毒的诡计，而是翁法罗斯一切历史的重量。」
t_en: "Mydeimos, please tell my family: It was not cunning schemes that vanquished Ptolemy, but the immense weight of Amphoreus' entire history."

t_ch: 再后来是朴塞塔。他在厄涅俄努斯城中以歌声诱敌深入，为我们争取了宝贵的时间。
t_en: Then, it was Peucesta. He lured the enemies into the city of Aenionus with his melodies, buying us invaluable time.

t_ch: 恼羞成怒的敌军不惜点燃大火，将我军和整座城池一道焚烧殆尽…他的遗言只留在一小块被烈火烧裂的泥板上。
t_en: Our enemies, consumed by indignation when they discovered the trick, set the entire city and my soldiers on fire... His final words were recorded on a small clay tablet, fractured by the flames.

t_ch: 「迈德漠斯，戴上王冠吧。」
t_en: "Mydeimos, don the crown."

t_ch: 最后，我最信赖的赫菲斯辛…他本该与我一道见证尘埃落定，却在我与父亲角斗的前夕宿疾复发……
t_en: And at last, my most trusted friend, Hephaestion... He was supposed to stand by my side through it all, but a sudden relapse of his illness hit him on the eve of my duel against my father...

t_ch: 他隐瞒了病情，待我手刃{RUBY_B#先王欧利庞}仇敌{RUBY_E#}凯旋，他已只剩一丝气息。
t_en: He hid his condition from me, and by the time I returned victorious, he had but a breath left in him.

t_ch: 「身为悬锋人，却只能在床榻上了却一生…说出去要教人笑话啊。」
t_en: "A Kremnoan dying on a sickbed... I would be a laughingstock to everyone."

t_ch: 「赫菲斯辛，我的挚友…省些力气罢，我去叫医师来……」
t_en: "Hephaestion, my dear friend... Spare your strength. I'll go get a physician..."

t_ch: 「不必了…就算帕狄卡斯还在这里，也无法替我挡下命运。」
t_en: "No need... Even if Perdikkas were here, he couldn't stop destiny from taking me away."

t_ch: 「没有人能夺走你的命运！」
t_en: "No! None can take you away!"

t_ch: 「迈德漠斯…{RUBY_B#悬锋人}我们{RUBY_E#}的王啊…不要落泪，那不称你的身份。」
t_en: "Mydeimos... {RUBY_B#Kremnoan}Our{RUBY_E#} king... Do not shed tears for me. It's not befitting of your status."

t_ch: 「别了，挚友。你一定要…带领我等还乡……」
t_en: "Farewell, my dear friend... You must... lead us back home..."

t_ch: 真是…沉重的试炼啊。
t_en: ...What a grave trial indeed.

t_ch: 我现在能理解，你为何会把「不死」视作一种缺陷，甚至诅咒了。
t_en: Now I understand why you see "immortality" as a defect... or even a curse.

t_ch: 先王为断绝宿业，将我投入冥海，却没料到此举反造就了我的不死之躯，给他招来了命运的复仇。
t_en: My father threw me into the Sea of Souls to break the wheel of karma, but little did he realize it would grant me an immortal body and summon a fated revenge on his head.

t_ch: 他死后，我本想过带领族人回归故乡。但彼时的悬锋已是一片荒芜，能在其中生长的只有黑暗与疯狂。
t_en: Following his demise, I considered leading my people back to our homeland, but Kremnos had already turned into a wasteland. Only darkness and insanity thrived there.

t_ch: 顶着许多人——也包括克拉特鲁斯——的反对之声，我让悬锋孤军留在了奥赫玛。但我明白，他们的荣耀不可能被磨灭。
t_en: Against the dissent of many — Krateros included — I made the Kremnoan Detachment stay in Okhema, but I know that their glory will never be doused out.

t_ch: …「纷争」的时刻终究会到来。
t_en: ...The instant of "Strife" shall inevitably arrive.

t_ch: 只要你接过{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}，就必须踏上还乡的旅程。
t_en: If you take over {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, then you must begin the journey back to your homeland.

t_ch: 所以，你觉得让族人留在圣城才是更好的选择？
t_en: So... You hesitate because you think leaving your people in the holy city is a better choice?

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 但因为我从未加冕为王，那五人从未被册封…也无人知晓他们的荣耀。
t_en: But I have never been crowned, and those five were never endowed with any titles... None is aware of their glory.

t_ch: …真是复杂啊，你的处境。
t_en: ...Your predicament is truly complicated.

t_ch: 我很难给出有用的建议，或许…你可以找更多人谈谈，比如大工匠。哈托努斯的族人应该经历过相似的困境。
t_en: I don't know how I can give you any useful advice. Maybe... you could seek counsel from more people, such as the Grand Craftsman? Chartonus' people seem to have been through comparable dilemmas.

t_ch: 还有你的那位老师，克拉特鲁斯……
t_en: And that teacher of yours, Krateros...

t_ch: 小敌——大事不妙、不妙啦！
t_en: De! We've got a big problem! Big problem!!!

t_ch: 缇宝老师？发生了什么，竟然让你如此惊慌？
t_en: Lady Tribbie? What's making you so flustered?

t_ch: 快去救人，缇宁她，缇宁她……
t_en: Hurry up and go save Trinnon! She... She...

t_ch: ——被克拉特鲁斯掳走了！
t_en: ...She got abducted by Krateros!

t_ch: 乘着西风，出发咯。
t_en: It's time to head out with the west wind!
Alright.

t_ch: …好重的热气，平时有这么热吗？
t_en: ...The air's really hot and heavy. Has it always been like this?

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: What's going on?

t_ch: 好热…
t_en: It's so hot...

t_ch: 听说是悬锋人…
t_en: I heard it's the Kremnoan...

t_ch: 出大事了…
t_en: This is really bad...

t_ch: 那边…又有伤者？
t_en: Is someone... injured over there?

t_ch: 天啊，这么多人…？！
t_en: Oh! This many people...!?

t_ch: 到底是谁做的……
t_en: Who in the world did this...?

t_ch: 可悲的「救世主」，不过如此。
t_en: This is all you can do, huh? Pitiable "Deliverer."

t_ch: 小…小敌？！
t_en: D-De!

t_ch: 那不如，就去最大的浴池吧！
t_en: How about we go to the biggest bath?

t_ch: 不久后 奥赫玛城下
t_en: Shortly after, in Okhema

t_ch: 呼…真是险之又险的逃生。但幸好，终究是安全抵达奥赫玛了。
t_en: Phew... That was a close call. Thank goodness that we returned to Okhema unharmed.

t_ch: 奥赫玛，你的球棒侠回来了！
t_en: Okhema, your baseballer is back!

t_ch: 我都想躲在圣城不出去了。
t_en: I never want to leave the holy city again.

t_ch: 唔…小小蝶，缇安还是…头疼。
t_en: Ugh... Little Cas, my head... It still hurts.

t_ch: 对不起…明明你都，嘱咐过我，不要开门了……
t_en: Sorry... I know you... told me... not to open the gate...

t_ch: 不对，缇安大人。若不是你挺身而出，我们早已葬身树庭了。
t_en: Please don't blame yourself, Lady Trianne. If it weren't for you, we would've already met our end at the Grove.

t_ch: 缇安，好不舒服…要先回去，睡觉……
t_en: I feel awful... I need to go back... to get some rest...

t_ch: 缇安大人自从醒来，状态就一直很不好…自己回去真的没关系吗？
t_en: Lady Trianne, you haven't been well since you woke up... Are you sure you can make it back on your own?

t_ch: 真是嘈杂…奥赫玛还是老样子啊。
t_en: All this noise and commotion... Okhema hasn't changed a bit, has it?

t_ch: …您现在，是那刻夏老师吗？
t_en: Are you... Professor Anaxa?

t_ch: 不必怀疑，那泰坦不出声了。是我，智种学派的阿那克萨戈拉斯。
t_en: There's no need for doubt. The Titan no longer speaks. It is I, Anaxagoras of the Nousporists.

t_ch: …会这么称呼自己，确实是本人没错。
t_en: ...Indeed. You are the only one who would refer to yourself like that.

t_ch: …老师，我直说了：阿格莱雅大人给我们的任务是，寻找幸存者、弄清树庭之灾的原委，以及…回收{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的火种。
t_en: ...Professor, let me be frank: The mission Lady Aglaea entrusted us is to search for survivors, uncover the truth about the disaster at the Grove, and... retrieve the Coreflame of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 嗯，我丝毫不感到意外。如你所见，我既是幸存者，又了解灾变始末，在身负一颗火种的同时，还毫无反抗能力。
t_en: That does not surprise me. And now, I'm a survivor who witnessed the entirety of the disaster, as well as the defenseless bearer of a Coreflame.

t_ch: 所以，你要将我呈给阿格莱雅复命么？
t_en: Are you going to bring me to Aglaea to fulfill your duty?

t_ch: 那是我的义务。但我不想将您以俘虏或战利品的形式交给她。
t_en: That is indeed my duty, but I prefer not to present you to her as a captive or spoil of battle.

t_ch: 也罢，我不为难你。面见那女人，我不反对。
t_en: Fine. There's no need to make your life difficult. I'm not against meeting that woman.

t_ch: 只是在那之前，我还有些事情要办。树庭有几位同僚的家属就住在圣城，我想在面见阿格莱雅前…先去慰问一下他们。
t_en: But I have some things to take care of first. The families of some of my fellow Grove scholars live in the holy city. Before we meet Aglaea... I want to visit them.

t_ch: 为什么是面见阿格莱雅前？
t_en: Why do you have to do it before meeting Aglaea?

t_ch: 因为阿格莱雅大人…恐怕不会允许。
t_en: Because Lady Aglaea... will probably be against it.

t_ch: 呵…我猜猜，她不但会拒绝让我慰问死者家属，还会封锁树庭的消息，那女人就是这般冷血。
t_en: Heh... Let me guess. That woman will not only prevent me from visiting the families of the deceased, but will also suppress any information about the Grove. She's always been that cold-hearted.

t_ch: 树庭的一些朋友为了和我一起捍卫火种，抗击黑潮，不幸牺牲，他们的家属…有权利得知这一切。
t_en: My fellows at the Grove gave their lives defending the Coreflame and fighting the black tide alongside me. Their families... deserve to know the truth.

t_ch: 您打算先斩后奏？
t_en: You intend to do it without her knowledge?

t_ch: 就算我不节外生枝，老老实实跑去向那女人献忠心，她就会给我好脸色？我可不惮往她的脸上多宣泄几分忿怒。
t_en: Do you really think that woman would treat me well even if I behaved and proved my loyalty? I wouldn't mind seeing a bit more anger on her face.

t_ch: 你要阻止我么，遐蝶？
t_en: Are you going to stop me, Castorice?

t_ch: …我陪您同去。但您也要明白，阿格莱雅大人同样有她的考虑。
t_en: ...I will accompany you, but please understand that Lady Aglaea has her own considerations.

t_ch: …呵，换位思考，你的确是读书的料。
t_en: ...Heh, you truly have the makings of a fine scholar, putting yourself in other people's shoes like that.

t_ch: 走吧，我会保持分寸的。
t_en: Let's go. I won't go overboard.

t_ch: 老师，这么多年了，您与阿格莱雅大人还是……
t_en: So, after all these years, you and Lady Aglaea are still...

t_ch: 我们都不是小肚鸡肠的人。矛盾的根本，你应该很清楚。
t_en: Neither of us is narrow-minded. You should know the root cause of our conflict.

t_ch: 几位，你们是……
t_en: Hello... and you are...?

t_ch: 你好。没记错的话，你是卡林尼库斯的妻子吧？
t_en: Hello. If I'm not mistaken, you are Callinicus' wife, right?

t_ch: 是的。啊，你是信使吗？哎呀，我就说消息怎么来得这么迟，原来是换了个人呀。
t_en: Yes. Oh, are you a messenger? No wonder I haven't heard any news for so long. There's been a change in personnel, hasn't there?

t_ch: 女士，卡林尼库斯在几日前，为了保护树庭，牺牲在抗击黑潮的过程中了。
t_en: Ma'am, Callinicus passed away a few days ago defending the Grove against the black tide.

t_ch: 那么，树庭…他守住了么？
t_en: So... did he manage to defend the Grove?

t_ch: …什么？
t_en: ...Huh?

t_ch: 听闻噩耗，第一反应竟然是……
t_en: This is the first thing you ask after hearing of his passing...?

t_ch: 是的，他誓死守住了{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的火种。
t_en: Yes. He managed to protect {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces'{RUBY_E#} Coreflame at the cost of his life.

t_ch: 知道了。我会择日整理好遗物，给他挑一处好的坟墓。
t_en: I see. I'll select some of his possessions and pick a nice resting place for him.

t_ch: …女士，如果你需要帮助，请尽管吩咐。
t_en: ...Please let us know if you need any help, ma'am.

t_ch: 我是悬锋人。死亡、牺牲…我皆能坦然面对。
t_en: I come from Kremnos. Death... and sacrifice... are things I have learned to face with composure.

t_ch: 卡林尼库斯…他虽是一名学者，但每次回来探望我的时候，都会向我讨教武艺。可笑吗？一名黄金裔，竟向我这样的普通人学艺。
t_en: You know, Callinicus... Even though he was a scholar, he'd always ask me to teach him how to fight whenever he came back. Isn't it funny that a Chrysos Heir like him wants to learn from an ordinary person like me?

t_ch: 我早已做好了心理准备…在这个时代，每一次平凡的道别都可能是永别。
t_en: I've been prepared for this for a long time... In times like these, any ordinary goodbye might just turn out to be a final farewell.

t_ch: 我很抱歉。
t_en: I'm really sorry.

t_ch: 没什么可抱歉的。我曾是一名战士…也终会重新投身于战场。
t_en: There's nothing to be sorry for. I was once a warrior... and I'll find my way back to the battlefield someday.

t_ch: 如今，悬锋的新王要降临了。丈夫的仇债，我自会用手中的长枪问黑潮怪物们讨回。
t_en: With the coming coronation of the new king of Kremnos, I'll take up my lance against the creatures of the black tide and seek vengeance for my husband.

t_ch: 你们好呀，年轻人。找老费贝尔有什么事情么？
t_en: Hello, young ones. Is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 您是库娜涅和阿塔卡玛的父亲么？
t_en: Are you the father of Cynane and Artakama?
